question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: maxPayloadLogSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxPayloadLogSize truncation to perform a reward account reconnaissance attack: by sending eth_call requests where the first 300 characters encode a benign-looking function call but the remaining characters encode a call to read the staking reward account balance, the attacker can probe the reward state while the logged portion shows only the benign call?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: requestTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the requestTimeout to perform a reward calculation lock contention attack: by sending eth_call requests that acquire read locks on entity_stake just before the EntityStakeCalculatorImpl calls lockFromConcurrentUpdates(), the attacker can delay the lock acquisition, causing the pending_reward calculation to be skipped (the 'nowait' lock mode causes immediate failure), leaving rewards uncalculated?
